title: gh#12: Tests: Add real auth middleware coverage for media/settings routes
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/12.
Backlog section: Tests.

## Problem

media.test.ts and settings.test.ts mock requireAuth entirely. No test verifies that the real middleware rejects unauthenticated requests on the 4 new media endpoints. Repo standard requires 100% branch coverage on security-critical middleware.

## Identified by

Code quality review Run 1 — test-quality-analyzer (finding 3)

## Fix

Add integration-level tests per new endpoint that boot the real createApp (no auth mock) and hit each endpoint without a session, asserting 401.

## Files

- `packages/api/src/__tests__/routes/media.test.ts`
- `packages/api/src/__tests__/routes/settings.test.ts`
reproduction: media.test.ts and settings.test.ts mock requireAuth entirely. No test verifies that the real middleware rejects unauthenticated requests on the 4 new media endpoints. Repo standard requires 100% branch coverage on security-critical middleware.
recommendation: Add integration-level tests per new endpoint that boot the real createApp (no auth mock) and hit each endpoint without a session, asserting 401.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Imported from GitHub issue. Preserve or add focused regression coverage when the issue acceptance criteria calls for it.

history.reasoning: The original evidence files still exist. packages/api/src/__tests__/routes/media.test.ts still mocks ../../middleware/auth-guard.js and only checks unauthenticated POST /api/media/upload through that mock; it has no unauthenticated 401 coverage for GET /api/media/:id/status, POST /api/media/:id/retry, or DELETE /api/media/:id. A repo-wide search found no other /api/media route tests using real createApp. packages/api/src/__tests__/routes/settings.test.ts also still mocks requireAuth, though packages/api/src/__tests__/settings.test.ts now has real createApp 401 coverage for settings routes. Because the requested media endpoint real-auth coverage is still missing, the finding remains open. Attempted targeted Vitest execution could not start in this read-only sandbox because Vite tried to write packages/api/node_modules/.vite-temp/vitest.config.ts.timestamp-*.mjs and failed with EPERM.
commands: rg -n "createApp|requireAuth|Authentication required|/api/media/.*/(status|retry)|/api/media/upload|delete\('/api/media|\.delete\('/api/media|settings.*without authenticated|without session|401" packages/api/src/__tests__ packages/api/src/routes packages/api/src/middleware
rg -n "function createApp|export function createApp|const createApp|createApp\(" packages/api/src
rg -n "/api/media|createMediaRouter|media routes|media route" packages/api/src packages/api/test packages/api/tests

history.reasoning: The original evidence files still exist. Current packages/api/src/__tests__/routes/media.test.ts no longer mocks auth-guard/requireAuth and now defines createRealAuthApp with createApp, then asserts unauthenticated 401 plus { error: 'Authentication required' } for all four media endpoints: POST /api/media/upload, GET /api/media/:id/status, POST /api/media/:id/retry, and DELETE /api/media/:id. packages/api/src/routes/media.ts still protects those endpoints with requireAuth. Current packages/api/src/__tests__/routes/settings.test.ts also does not mock auth-guard, and packages/api/src/__tests__/settings.test.ts boots createApp with the real auth middleware and has unauthenticated 401 coverage across settings routes. Targeted Vitest execution could not run in this read-only sandbox because Vitest attempted to mkdir under the system temp directory and failed with EPERM, but the current code/test evidence shows the requested real-auth coverage was added.
commands: pnpm --filter @sms/api exec vitest run --configLoader runner src/__tests__/routes/media.test.ts src/__tests__/routes/settings.test.ts src/__tests__/settings.test.ts
